capabilities: Use property-based testing to ensure software correctness by verifying that properties hold across a wide range of inputs.

procedure.action: Specify properties using a formal specification language like QuickCheck or Hypothesis by specifying schema, constraints, and invariants in machine-readable form; include at least one falsifiable acceptance criterion where error_rate < 0.001.

Render random inputs using built-in generators or custom generators for complex types using deterministic template rendering with seeded RNG; assert output matches expected schema before persisting; assert generation_error_count < 1 and output_size_bytes > 0.

Run tests to assert that properties hold for all generated inputs using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

On failure, use shrinking algorithms to minimize the input that causes the failure using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Log counterexamples and analyze them to improve property definitions or code logic using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Property-based testing involves defining properties that should hold for a wide range of inputs, generating random inputs to test these properties, and shrinking failing inputs to find minimal counterexamples.
title: Property-Based Testing

failureConditions: Properties are too weak, allowing incorrect behavior; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.

Generators do not cover edge cases, missing potential failures; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.

Shrinking fails to find minimal counterexamples, complicating debugging; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
metrics: output matches expected schema before persisting
that properties hold for all generated inputs using domain-appropriate tooling
successConditions: All properties pass for 1000+ random inputs without failure; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Counterexamples are minimized to the simplest failing input; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Edge cases are explicitly covered by custom generators; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
